sorting-direction.label: Sorting Direction (ASC, DESC)
sorting-direction.summary: Control sort order with ASC and DESC keywords.

sk1.title: Sorting Results in SQL
sk1.bodyMarkdown: When you want to sort the results of a SQL query, you use the `ORDER BY` clause. By default, sorting is in ascending order (from smallest to largest or A to Z). To control the direction, add `ASC` for ascending or `DESC` for descending after the column name.

sk2.title: ASC and DESC Keywords
sk2.bodyMarkdown: `ASC` stands for ascending order, and `DESC` stands for descending order. You can specify these keywords for each column in your `ORDER BY` clause. For example: `ORDER BY price DESC` will show the highest prices first.

q1.title: Default Sorting Direction
q1.prompt: What is the default sorting direction when you use ORDER BY in SQL without specifying ASC or DESC?
q1.hint: Think about what happens if you don't specify a direction.

help.concept: ORDER BY sorts results in ascending order by default unless you specify DESC.
help.hint_1: If you don't write ASC or DESC, SQL will sort from lowest to highest.
help.hint_2: Ascending order is the default for ORDER BY.

q2.title: Using ASC and DESC
q2.prompt: Which of the following ORDER BY clauses will sort the 'price' column from highest to lowest?
q2.hint: Look for the keyword that means descending.

help.concept: DESC sorts values from highest to lowest, while ASC sorts from lowest to highest.
help.hint_1: Only one keyword means descending order.
help.hint_2: DESC is the keyword for sorting from highest to lowest.

q3.title: Fill in the Sorting Keyword
q3.prompt:
Complete the SQL statement to sort students by their score from highest to lowest:

SELECT * FROM students_intro ORDER BY score ____;
q3.hint: Which keyword sorts in reverse order?

help.concept: the missing term is used to sort in descending order.
help.hint_1: ASC is for ascending, but you want the highest scores first.
help.hint_2: the missing term is the keyword for descending order.

q3.template: SELECT * FROM students_intro ORDER BY score ____;

q4.title: Ordering SQL Clauses
q4.prompt: Arrange the SQL keywords to create a query that selects all products and sorts them by name in descending order.
q4.hint: Start with SELECT, then FROM, then ORDER BY.

help.concept: The correct order of SQL clauses is SELECT, FROM, ORDER BY, then the sorting direction.
help.hint_1: ORDER BY comes after FROM, and DESC is attached to the column.
help.hint_2: DESC should follow the ORDER BY column.

q5.title: Sort Employees by Name (Descending)
q5.prompt: Write a SQL query to select all columns from the employees_basic table and sort the results by the 'name' column in descending order.
q5.hint: Use ORDER BY and the correct keyword for descending order.

help.concept: ORDER BY lets you sort results, and DESC sorts in descending order.
help.hint_1: After SELECT and FROM, use ORDER BY with the column and DESC.
help.hint_2: ORDER BY name DESC will sort names from Z to A.
